item.title: Motorola Razr (2023) vs Motorola Razr+: Which foldable Moto is for you?
item.itunes:title: Motorola Razr (2023) vs Motorola Razr+: Which foldable Moto is for you?
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
• Source: Motorola
    Motorola Razr (2023)
    $600 $700 Save $100

    The 2023 Motorola Razr is the newest foldable phone on the market and offers an ultra-compact and lightweight clamshell design. It offers a smaller pOLED outer display than the Razr+ but it has a refresh rate of 144Hz, making it great for gaming. It runs on a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset.

    Pros
    • Larger outer display
    • Upgraded chipset
    • 4K 60fps videos
    Cons
    • Pricey
    • Weak battery life
    $600 at Motorola
  • Motorola Razr+ (2023)
    $800 $1000 Save $200

    The Motorola Razr+ (2023) is an upgraded version of the base Motorola Razr from this year. The large 3.6-inch pOLED outer display allows for more use without having to open the clamshell design. It offers 256GB of storage, multiple camera angles, and the inner display has a refresh rate of 165Hz.

    Pros
    • Larger outer display
    • More storage
    • Better chipset
    Cons
    • Smaller battery
    • Weaker rear camera
    $800 at Amazon

When Motorola announced a new Razr lineup, people wondered when they might debut in 2023. The Motorola Razr+ came out at the beginning of the summer and was an immediate hit in the foldable smartphone market. At XDA, we quickly dubbed it "the best foldable Motorola phone" because of its sleek design and larger outer display. There's so much that you can do with it, but that might not be what everyone wants. Enter the base model, Motorola Razr (2023). This is coming out this week and is a more affordable version of the Razr. Which one is better for you? Let's help you decide by comparing them.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Price, specs & availability

Despite being announced at the same time, it wasn't known when each of these would come out. The Motorola Razr+ made its debut earlier and was announced for the market price of $1,000. That puts it at a more affordable comparison of the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 5, Samsung's latest flip phone. It's available at major retailers like Amazon for an unlocked version. But you can get one at Motorola for $800 if you trade in a phone. It offers 256GB of storage, 8GB of RAM, and a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 chipset.

This year's version of the Razr is available on October 20th but available for preordering now. You can choose one from Motorola and get it for either $700 on its own or $600 if you have a trade-in. You can buy an unlocked version from Amazon for $700. It offers 128GB of storage, 8GB of RAM, and a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset.

  • Motorola Razr (2023)
    SoC
    Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 Gen 1
    Display
    6.9-inch FHD pOLED inner display, 1.5-inch OLED outer display
    RAM
    8GB
    Storage
    128GB
    Battery
    4200mAh
    Ports
    USB-C 2.0
    Operating System
    Android 13
    Front camera
    32MP selfie camera 32MP (f/2.4, 0.7µm or 1.4µm@8MP Quad Pixel)
    Rear camera
    64MP (f/1.7, 0.7µm or 16MP 1.4µm Quad Pixel
    Colors
    Sage Green, Summer Lilac, Vanilla Cream, Cherry Blossom
    Weight
    188.6g
    Charge speed
    30W TurboPower™ charging support
    Price
    $700
  • Motorola Razr+ (2023)
    SoC
    Qualcomm Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1
    Display
    6.9-inch 165Hz OLED (inner); 3.6-inch 144Hz OLED (outer)
    RAM
    8GB
    Storage
    256GB UFS 3.1
    Battery
    3,800 mAh
    Ports
    USB-C
    Operating System
    Android 13
    Front camera
    32MP
    Rear camera
    12MP, f/1.5 wide; 13MP f2/2 ultra-wide
    Colors
    Black, red, blue
    Weight
    188g
    Charge speed
    30W wired; 5W wireless
    Price
    $1,000

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Design

The two phones look rather similar from the outside. Motorola promised a gapless design and that works for both phones. There are slightly raised bezels, so you're able to fold the phone but don't lose much when unfolding it to see the inner display. The Razr+ comes in three different colors: Infinite Black, Glacier Blue, and Viva Magenta. Viva Magenta is only available through T-Mobile or on Motorola.com. Viva Magenta actually weighs four grams less than the other two, which clock in at 188.5 grams. Infinite Black and Glacier Blue have Corning Gorilla Glass Victus back and front with an aluminum frame. The Viva Magenta has a glass front and a vegan leather back and aluminum frame. All of them have an IP52 water-repellent design.

The Razr comes in four different colors: Sage Green, Summer Lilac, Vanilla Cream, and Cherry Blossom. None are exclusive to a specific carrier and all are available at Motorola and Amazon. It weighs more than any of the Razr+, but you likely won't tell the difference. All colors are 188.6 grams in weight. The Razr is similar in construction to the Viva Magenta, as it has Corning Gorilla Glass Victus back, an aluminum frame, and vegan leather finishing. Motorola claims this has a water-repellent design, but since it is not stated as an IP52-rated design, it's hard to know just how much water it can withstand. Both the Razr and the Razr+ have a USB-C 2.0 port for charging. They also both have a fingerprint sensor and face unlock for security purposes. Neither have a headphone jack, so you'll need Bluetooth headphones if you want to connect. Both offer dual stereo speakers with Dolby Atmos and Spatial Sound.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Display

Arguably the biggest difference between the two phones is the outer display. On the Razr+, it offers a 3.6-inch pOLED display that covers the front and around the cameras. Not only is it large and allows you to check notifications or the time, but you can do so much more with it. It actually looks like a smartphone display that's stretched across the cover. You can play games on it, so you won't even have to open the phone. It is a 1066 x 1056 display with a 144Hz refresh rate. It can reach a peak brightness of 1,100 nits for just the external display. You can customize the widgets on the external display to be ones that you're going to use frequently. Plus, there's a continue button on the screen that allows you to continue using an app that you were using with the phone open.

The external display of the Razr is a 1.5-inch OLED display that allows you to utilize some widgets and look at notifications. The purpose of the smaller display is to set up people who may not want to be looking at their phone all the time. It is limited in its capabilities and that will be fine for some people. It only offers a resolution of 194 x 368.

Both of these phones offer the same size internal display. It is a 6.9-inch FHD+ pOLED display but the refresh rate of the Razr+ is 165Hz and the refresh rate of the Razr is 144Hz. So you'll get a smoother looking display with the Razr+. The aspect ratio is 22:9 on both. The colors and brightness are just a bit better on the Razr+.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Software and performance

Source: Motorola

The chipset in the Razr+ is a more updated version than the Razr. Both utilize Qualcomm Snapdragon SoC, but the Razr+ has a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 platform while the Razr has a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset. These are both previous generation chipsets, as they are not the most up-to-date versions on the market. The Razr+ is just a bit faster and smoother than the Razr. While this may not bother some people, especially if you aren't using your phone for gaming or streaming, others will be affected by it, so they'll likely want the newer chipset. Both run on an Android 13 operating system.

For performance, you're going to get more storage in the Razr+, giving you more possibilities to utilize both of your displays. It naturally makes sense that you're going to want the Razr+ if you're going to want to do more with your phone. The Razr+ offers 256GB of storage and 8GB of RAM while you're only getting 128GB of storage in the Razr. Both phones can be controlled with voice commands with Google Assistant.

The Razr offers a setting called Moto Unplugged that lets you choose different apps and notifications that you want to utilize or be alerted of at certain times of the day. You can set timers on when you want to see some of the notifications and apps, after which they'll go away when their time is up. This is currently only available on the Razr but will soon be available on the Razr+.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Battery Life

Source: Motorola

The storage of the Razr+ uses UFS 3.1, which causes the battery life to drain more. The battery is 3,800mAh, which is fine. But it doesn't last as long as you may want for a smartphone. But even though the battery may be a little bit behind other foldable phones, it makes up for it with 30W TurboPower charging support and 5W wireless charging. That will help keep your phone charged and ready to be used when you need it.

An upgrade that the Razr has is a larger 4,200mAh battery. But the storage takes up more power, as it uses UFS 2.2. It all balances out, as this also has 30W TurboPower charging support and 5W wireless charging. We'll wait and see how long it supposedly lasts, but Motorola claims it will last an entire day on a single charge.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Camera

Thanks to the unique design of foldable phones, you more or less have a built-in tripod whenever you try and take a picture. That's no exception with these two phones, as they can be placed between 45 and 130 degrees in order to snap a photo. They both offer a dual-view features that will let both the photographer and the subject see what the photo is setting up to look like. Both phones have a 32MP front selfie camera, which is a step up from previous Razr lineups. The main sensor on Razr+ can also record 4K 60FPS video, but neither has the ability to shoot that in ultrawide. The rear camera on the Razr+ is a 12MP one that has an aperture of f/1.5, which can result in some blurrier photos if taken during the day as it takes in more light for a shallower field of view. This isn't a camera that will blow anyone away, especially if you're using the 13MP ultrawide sensor with f/2.2 aperture that we noted takes bad nighttime photos.

The Razr has a 64MP rear camera with an aperture of f/1.7, meaning it takes in even more light with an even shallower field of view. So it has a larger sensor but can be prone to overexposing photos or creating blurrier ones. On your small display, you may not notice the details that the larger sensor takes in, but on a larger computer screen, you'll notice them. The ultrawide sensor is 16MP with Quad Pixel technology, so it helps the photos taken at nighttime. You'll get more clarity at night with the Razr than you will the Razr+.

Which is right for you?

You may think that the Razr and the Razr+ are similar phones, and you'd be right in some aspects. But there are subtle differences that make one the superior phone. That doesn't mean that there isn't a place for the other one, especially if someone is looking for a less expensive phone. But the Razr+ is our pick for the better Motorola Razr phone right now.

It has an upgraded chipset than the Razr and has more storage, allowing you to do more. But the biggest plus in its corner is th larger outer display that you can utilize for all kinds of features. The capabilities of that outer display is what gives this phone the edge.

Motorola Razr+ (2023)
Editor's choice
$800 $1000 Save $200

The Motorola Razr+ (2023) is a clamshell phone with a 3.6-inch outer display that allows you to handle many features without having to open the phone.

$800 at Amazon

But the Motorola Razr, which is being released this week, is worth it for people who may not want to spend $1,000. You can snag this for only $700, and it has a lot of great features too. The larger battery, the upgrades to the camera, and the similarities that it has with the Razr+ all work in its favor. It's great for people who may not want to use their phone constantly but want a capable clamshell design phone that you can use every day.

Source: Motorola
Motorola Razr (2023)
Best value
$600 $700 Save $100

The Motorola Razr (2023) is ultra-compact and is an affordable clamshell design phone. The large battery and quality camera features make this a phone for anyone that is looking for a budget-friendly foldable.

$600 at Motorola
item.description:
• Source: Motorola
    Motorola Razr (2023)
    $600 $700 Save $100

    The 2023 Motorola Razr is the newest foldable phone on the market and offers an ultra-compact and lightweight clamshell design. It offers a smaller pOLED outer display than the Razr+ but it has a refresh rate of 144Hz, making it great for gaming. It runs on a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset.

    Pros
    • Larger outer display
    • Upgraded chipset
    • 4K 60fps videos
    Cons
    • Pricey
    • Weak battery life
    $600 at Motorola
  • Motorola Razr+ (2023)
    $800 $1000 Save $200

    The Motorola Razr+ (2023) is an upgraded version of the base Motorola Razr from this year. The large 3.6-inch pOLED outer display allows for more use without having to open the clamshell design. It offers 256GB of storage, multiple camera angles, and the inner display has a refresh rate of 165Hz.

    Pros
    • Larger outer display
    • More storage
    • Better chipset
    Cons
    • Smaller battery
    • Weaker rear camera
    $800 at Amazon

When Motorola announced a new Razr lineup, people wondered when they might debut in 2023. The Motorola Razr+ came out at the beginning of the summer and was an immediate hit in the foldable smartphone market. At XDA, we quickly dubbed it "the best foldable Motorola phone" because of its sleek design and larger outer display. There's so much that you can do with it, but that might not be what everyone wants. Enter the base model, Motorola Razr (2023). This is coming out this week and is a more affordable version of the Razr. Which one is better for you? Let's help you decide by comparing them.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Price, specs & availability

Despite being announced at the same time, it wasn't known when each of these would come out. The Motorola Razr+ made its debut earlier and was announced for the market price of $1,000. That puts it at a more affordable comparison of the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 5, Samsung's latest flip phone. It's available at major retailers like Amazon for an unlocked version. But you can get one at Motorola for $800 if you trade in a phone. It offers 256GB of storage, 8GB of RAM, and a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 chipset.

This year's version of the Razr is available on October 20th but available for preordering now. You can choose one from Motorola and get it for either $700 on its own or $600 if you have a trade-in. You can buy an unlocked version from Amazon for $700. It offers 128GB of storage, 8GB of RAM, and a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset.

  • Motorola Razr (2023)
    SoC
    Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 Gen 1
    Display
    6.9-inch FHD pOLED inner display, 1.5-inch OLED outer display
    RAM
    8GB
    Storage
    128GB
    Battery
    4200mAh
    Ports
    USB-C 2.0
    Operating System
    Android 13
    Front camera
    32MP selfie camera 32MP (f/2.4, 0.7µm or 1.4µm@8MP Quad Pixel)
    Rear camera
    64MP (f/1.7, 0.7µm or 16MP 1.4µm Quad Pixel
    Colors
    Sage Green, Summer Lilac, Vanilla Cream, Cherry Blossom
    Weight
    188.6g
    Charge speed
    30W TurboPower™ charging support
    Price
    $700
  • Motorola Razr+ (2023)
    SoC
    Qualcomm Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1
    Display
    6.9-inch 165Hz OLED (inner); 3.6-inch 144Hz OLED (outer)
    RAM
    8GB
    Storage
    256GB UFS 3.1
    Battery
    3,800 mAh
    Ports
    USB-C
    Operating System
    Android 13
    Front camera
    32MP
    Rear camera
    12MP, f/1.5 wide; 13MP f2/2 ultra-wide
    Colors
    Black, red, blue
    Weight
    188g
    Charge speed
    30W wired; 5W wireless
    Price
    $1,000

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Design

The two phones look rather similar from the outside. Motorola promised a gapless design and that works for both phones. There are slightly raised bezels, so you're able to fold the phone but don't lose much when unfolding it to see the inner display. The Razr+ comes in three different colors: Infinite Black, Glacier Blue, and Viva Magenta. Viva Magenta is only available through T-Mobile or on Motorola.com. Viva Magenta actually weighs four grams less than the other two, which clock in at 188.5 grams. Infinite Black and Glacier Blue have Corning Gorilla Glass Victus back and front with an aluminum frame. The Viva Magenta has a glass front and a vegan leather back and aluminum frame. All of them have an IP52 water-repellent design.

The Razr comes in four different colors: Sage Green, Summer Lilac, Vanilla Cream, and Cherry Blossom. None are exclusive to a specific carrier and all are available at Motorola and Amazon. It weighs more than any of the Razr+, but you likely won't tell the difference. All colors are 188.6 grams in weight. The Razr is similar in construction to the Viva Magenta, as it has Corning Gorilla Glass Victus back, an aluminum frame, and vegan leather finishing. Motorola claims this has a water-repellent design, but since it is not stated as an IP52-rated design, it's hard to know just how much water it can withstand. Both the Razr and the Razr+ have a USB-C 2.0 port for charging. They also both have a fingerprint sensor and face unlock for security purposes. Neither have a headphone jack, so you'll need Bluetooth headphones if you want to connect. Both offer dual stereo speakers with Dolby Atmos and Spatial Sound.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Display

Arguably the biggest difference between the two phones is the outer display. On the Razr+, it offers a 3.6-inch pOLED display that covers the front and around the cameras. Not only is it large and allows you to check notifications or the time, but you can do so much more with it. It actually looks like a smartphone display that's stretched across the cover. You can play games on it, so you won't even have to open the phone. It is a 1066 x 1056 display with a 144Hz refresh rate. It can reach a peak brightness of 1,100 nits for just the external display. You can customize the widgets on the external display to be ones that you're going to use frequently. Plus, there's a continue button on the screen that allows you to continue using an app that you were using with the phone open.

The external display of the Razr is a 1.5-inch OLED display that allows you to utilize some widgets and look at notifications. The purpose of the smaller display is to set up people who may not want to be looking at their phone all the time. It is limited in its capabilities and that will be fine for some people. It only offers a resolution of 194 x 368.

Both of these phones offer the same size internal display. It is a 6.9-inch FHD+ pOLED display but the refresh rate of the Razr+ is 165Hz and the refresh rate of the Razr is 144Hz. So you'll get a smoother looking display with the Razr+. The aspect ratio is 22:9 on both. The colors and brightness are just a bit better on the Razr+.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Software and performance

Source: Motorola

The chipset in the Razr+ is a more updated version than the Razr. Both utilize Qualcomm Snapdragon SoC, but the Razr+ has a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 platform while the Razr has a Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 chipset. These are both previous generation chipsets, as they are not the most up-to-date versions on the market. The Razr+ is just a bit faster and smoother than the Razr. While this may not bother some people, especially if you aren't using your phone for gaming or streaming, others will be affected by it, so they'll likely want the newer chipset. Both run on an Android 13 operating system.

For performance, you're going to get more storage in the Razr+, giving you more possibilities to utilize both of your displays. It naturally makes sense that you're going to want the Razr+ if you're going to want to do more with your phone. The Razr+ offers 256GB of storage and 8GB of RAM while you're only getting 128GB of storage in the Razr. Both phones can be controlled with voice commands with Google Assistant.

The Razr offers a setting called Moto Unplugged that lets you choose different apps and notifications that you want to utilize or be alerted of at certain times of the day. You can set timers on when you want to see some of the notifications and apps, after which they'll go away when their time is up. This is currently only available on the Razr but will soon be available on the Razr+.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Battery Life

Source: Motorola

The storage of the Razr+ uses UFS 3.1, which causes the battery life to drain more. The battery is 3,800mAh, which is fine. But it doesn't last as long as you may want for a smartphone. But even though the battery may be a little bit behind other foldable phones, it makes up for it with 30W TurboPower charging support and 5W wireless charging. That will help keep your phone charged and ready to be used when you need it.

An upgrade that the Razr has is a larger 4,200mAh battery. But the storage takes up more power, as it uses UFS 2.2. It all balances out, as this also has 30W TurboPower charging support and 5W wireless charging. We'll wait and see how long it supposedly lasts, but Motorola claims it will last an entire day on a single charge.

Motorola Razr+ vs. Razr (2023): Camera

Thanks to the unique design of foldable phones, you more or less have a built-in tripod whenever you try and take a picture. That's no exception with these two phones, as they can be placed between 45 and 130 degrees in order to snap a photo. They both offer a dual-view features that will let both the photographer and the subject see what the photo is setting up to look like. Both phones have a 32MP front selfie camera, which is a step up from previous Razr lineups. The main sensor on Razr+ can also record 4K 60FPS video, but neither has the ability to shoot that in ultrawide. The rear camera on the Razr+ is a 12MP one that has an aperture of f/1.5, which can result in some blurrier photos if taken during the day as it takes in more light for a shallower field of view. This isn't a camera that will blow anyone away, especially if you're using the 13MP ultrawide sensor with f/2.2 aperture that we noted takes bad nighttime photos.

The Razr has a 64MP rear camera with an aperture of f/1.7, meaning it takes in even more light with an even shallower field of view. So it has a larger sensor but can be prone to overexposing photos or creating blurrier ones. On your small display, you may not notice the details that the larger sensor takes in, but on a larger computer screen, you'll notice them. The ultrawide sensor is 16MP with Quad Pixel technology, so it helps the photos taken at nighttime. You'll get more clarity at night with the Razr than you will the Razr+.

Which is right for you?

You may think that the Razr and the Razr+ are similar phones, and you'd be right in some aspects. But there are subtle differences that make one the superior phone. That doesn't mean that there isn't a place for the other one, especially if someone is looking for a less expensive phone. But the Razr+ is our pick for the better Motorola Razr phone right now.

It has an upgraded chipset than the Razr and has more storage, allowing you to do more. But the biggest plus in its corner is th larger outer display that you can utilize for all kinds of features. The capabilities of that outer display is what gives this phone the edge.

Motorola Razr+ (2023)
Editor's choice
$800 $1000 Save $200

The Motorola Razr+ (2023) is a clamshell phone with a 3.6-inch outer display that allows you to handle many features without having to open the phone.

$800 at Amazon

But the Motorola Razr, which is being released this week, is worth it for people who may not want to spend $1,000. You can snag this for only $700, and it has a lot of great features too. The larger battery, the upgrades to the camera, and the similarities that it has with the Razr+ all work in its favor. It's great for people who may not want to use their phone constantly but want a capable clamshell design phone that you can use every day.

Source: Motorola
Motorola Razr (2023)
Best value
$600 $700 Save $100

The Motorola Razr (2023) is ultra-compact and is an affordable clamshell design phone. The large battery and quality camera features make this a phone for anyone that is looking for a budget-friendly foldable.

$600 at Motorola

item.itunes:summary:
Minecraft has become quite a popular game since it was officially released in 2011, and thanks to the variety of mods and variations, it has a solid fan base around the world. Since it doesn't have any set goals or instructions, it is often described as a game with no rules, making it perfect for individuals across age groups to enjoy. Best of all, Minecraft is available on almost every single platform you can think of, including Windows, Mac, Linux, phones, tablets, consoles, and even the Raspberry Pi.

While you might think that practically every good laptop should support the game, the answer is not as straightforward, especially if you want to enjoy ray tracing. Despite its blocky look, Minecraft can take up a lot of PC resources. So if you want to play, check out the laptops below for options.

  • Source: Acer
    Acer Nitro 5
    Best overall gaming laptop for Minecraft
    $1242 at Amazon
  • HP Chromebook Plus x360 14-inch
    Best Chromebook for Minecraft
    $790 at HP
  • Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
    Best productivity laptop
    $1050 at Best Buy
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16 (Gen 8)
    Portable 16-inch gaming laptop
    $1300 at Lenovo
  • Source: HP
    HP Omen Transcend 16 (2023)
    The most powerful gaming laptop
    $1700 at HP

Our picks for the best laptops for Minecraft in 2023

Source: Acer
Acer Nitro 5
Best overall gaming laptop for Minecraft

Plenty of good features

The Acer Nitro 5 is a very capable gaming laptop for playing Minecraft. It has solid specs out of the box, including 13th-generation Intel CPUs, and a Nvidia GPU that supports Ray Tracing to make your Minecraft worlds more realistic.

Pros
  • Super easy to find and is relatively affordable
  • Has a great 12th-generation Intel CPU
  • Has a modern Nvidia GPU
Cons
  • Still has a FHD screen
$1242 at Amazon

Topping our list is the Acer Nitro 5 gaming laptop. This is quite a popular gaming laptop for mainstream gaming. It comes with everything you'll need to play Minecraft, along with games like Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020. Just be warned, it's not a cheap gaming laptop, and just a little bit over $1,000.

On the inside are the 12-generation Intel processors — specifically, an Intel Core i7-12650H CPU with 10 cores and 16 threads. The CPU can also boost up to 4.7 GHz for gaming. That's then paired up with the Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 16GB of RAM, and 1TBB SSD. This is all well beyond the suggested specs for Minecraft, so you can enjoy ray tracing and stutter-free gameplay. You also can push this laptop beyond the basics and use it for video editing, and other more intense games thanks to that GPU.

The display is another reason why we love this laptop. It has a 144Hz refresh rate, ensuring that your in-game movements, like breaking bricks and picking up objects, will look and feel smooth. It only has FHD resolution, but that's typical for this price range and is still more than good enough for a game like Minecraft, which isn't too graphically demanding. For more screen resolution, you always can connect to an external monitor.

Finally, we love the way this laptop looks. There are red accents on the corner of the laptop, four-zone RGB lighting on the keyboard, and transparent edges on the keycaps. You definitely can play Minecraft in style on this laptop, and showcase your laptop to your friends with plenty of pride.

HP Chromebook Plus x360 14-inch
Best Chromebook for Minecraft

Not over the top

You don't always need a regular gaming laptop for Minecraft. If you want to spend less than $1,000 on a laptop for Minecraft, you can enjoy the HP Chromebook Plus x360 14-inch, which runs the Android version of the game.

Pros
  • Runs Android version of Minecraft just fine
  • Under $1,0000
  • Convertible with a touch screen
Cons
  • ChromeOS has learning curves
$790 at HP

When you don't have $1,000 to spend on a gaming laptop just for Minecraft, you might want to consider a Chromebook instead. Chromebooks run the Android version of the game just fine. The Android version of Minecraft is compatible with cross-play with the other PC version of the title. This particular Chromebook is also one of the newest from Google's partner, HP, and it packs a touch screen for enjoying the game without a keyboard and mouse.

HP Chromebook Plus 14-inch is one of the Chromebook Plus models announced by Google. It packs in a CPU that's twice as fast as what's found on budget Chromebooks. It also has a display with better resolution, and a great webcam, too. It even supports pen input. All of these things combine to end up great for playing Minecraft. The device has an Intel Core i3-1215U CPU, which is well above the CPUs suggested for the Android version of the game. It also has 8GB of RAM and 128GB of storage. Specs like this ensure you not only can play Minecraft but also any other Android game you can think of, and enjoy multitasking, too.

The display on this Chromebook, a 14-inch 1920x1200 resolution screen can also bring your Minecraft worlds to life. It has a brightness of 250 nits, and the high resolution means you can see more of the world on your screen. Generally, this Chromebook is fancy, too. Since it has a mineral silver aluminum cover and feels premium. You're not cheating out if you want to play Minecraft in style without over-the-top features like an RGB keyboard.

Acer Swift Edge 16 (2023)
Best productivity laptop

For work and play

$1050 $1300 Save $250

The Acer Swift Edge 16 is a fast laptop with solid specs for playing Minecraft. It's not a gaming laptop, but with Minecraft being a lightweight game, this laptop can play it on lower settings without many issues. It has a great design, lots of ports for accessories, and a sleek OLED display with super slim bezels.

Pros
  • Has a great OLED screen
  • Has fast integrated graphics
  • Great design and connectivity
Cons
  • Can get expensive
$1050 at Best Buy

Sometimes, you don't need a laptop with a dedicated GPU to play Minecraft. If you need a laptop for work tasks and don't mind playing on lower settings, the Acer Swift Edge 16 is a great option. This laptop is powered by the AMD Ryzen 7 7840U, which is an eight-core processor. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and 1TB SSD. These specifications are well within the suggested range to run Minecraft, and thanks to the new RDNA3-based Radeon 780M integrated graphics, you shouldn't run into any issues.

The more important thing we want to mention is that this is a productivity laptop, so you can take it with you to school or work. It comes in a basic black color without the bells and whistles you usually see with gaming laptops. It also has a large 16-inch OLED display with slightly slim bezels. OLED panels are more vibrant than regular IPS and LCD panels, so it means your Minecraft worlds will end up looking more life-like.

The ports are what we love about this laptop, though. If you plan to use it at home for gaming or for extra screen space, there's a full-size HDMI port, for connections to an external display. That's in addition to USB-C, USB-A, a microSD card slot, and even a 1440p webcam.

Source: Lenovo
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16 (Gen 8)
Portable 16-inch gaming laptop

Thin but powerful

Lenovo's Legion Slim 5 (Gen 8) is a great laptop for Minecraft. It is under $1,500 and features AMD CPU options, and it has a great design and features like RGB lighting on the keyboard. Most importantly, it's thin but powerful.

$1300 at Lenovo $1530 at Best Buy

If you need a quality but portable 16-inch gaming laptop to play Minecraft look no further than the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16. This laptop sits under Lenovo's Legion Pro devices, but it's still plenty powerful for playing games. It's an AMD-powered laptop, too, that's upgradable. And, it packs in the power of RTX 4060 laptop graphics.

When it comes to the weight and dimensions, this is where this gaming laptop will win out. It comes in at 14.16 inches in length, 10.25 inches in width, and about 0.78 inches in thickness. It's also under 5 pounds. You can easily lug this laptop around, mainly because it's made of plastic. The laptop also has sleek design elements like plastic accents, an intake grille, and an aluminum lid cover.

As for what's under the hood of this laptop, it's AMD Ryzen CPUS. You can go up to an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS Processor and can add as much as 16GB RAM, and a 1TB SSD. That's also paired with up to an Nvidia RTX 4060 laptop GPU. All of his is above and beyond for a simple game like Minecraft. When we reviewed it, we got up to 79 FPS in games like Red Dead Redemption 2. The display is a huge win, too. It goes up to as much as QHD+ resolution at up to 240 Hz. Such a fast refresh rate means you can stay in the action as you build your worlds and unleash your creativity.

Source: HP
HP Omen Transcend 16 (2023)
The most powerful gaming laptop

All the speed you need

The Omen Transcend 16 (2023) is one of the most expensive gaming laptops, but it's the best at gaming> It also has a 16:10 mini-LED display option and strong CPU performance for games like Minecraft.

Pros
  • Has mini LED display
  • 13th-generation Intel CPUs
  • Great design
Cons
  • Still has a chin on the display
  • Crazy expensive
$1700 at HP $2300 at Best Buy $2050 at Amazon

Our last pick is actually one of the best possible laptops for playing games and for any task, but especially for Minecraft. You'll get the very best gaming experience, and so much more, especially if you're planning to use your laptop for other creative tasks.

For starters, the HP Omen Transcend 16 has one of the higher-end CPUs from Intel. It is up to the Intel Core i9-13900HX which has a crazy 24 Core CPU count. That's paired up with up to 32GB of RAM, and also Nvidia RTX 4060 graphics. When we tested this laptop, we got superfast framerates in all the games on our test library, Advancements in Nvidia's next-generation GPUs like the newer Nvidia Lovelace Architecture only helped the performance of this laptop.

Other than the CPU, you'll really display on this device. The 16:10 aspect ratio, as well as the 2560x1600 resolution panel, will help content look crisp and clear. It shouldn't be an issue for running Minecraft, either. In fact, this is one of the higher-resolution displays on any of the laptops on our list. You can even pick a mini LED panel, which offers more accurate colors.

The overall non-gaming style design, as well as the ports, cap off the reasons why we suggest this device. The HP Poem Transcend 16 has a great keyboard with RGB lighting, but a simplistic toned-down black exterior that's similar to a productivity laptop. As for ports, there are ports on the left and right side of the laptop, as well as the rear. You can easily connect this up to a display or any peripherals you might own thanks to the Thunderbolt 4 ports, HDMU ports, Ethernet ports, and USB-A ports.

What you need to know about Minecraft on PC

There are technically three editions of the game for PC. There is the classic Java Edition of the game, the Bedrock Edition, which expanded support to platforms that don't support Java and Minecraft for Windows. Note that as of June 2022, the Bedrock Edition and the Java Edition have merged into one version, which you'll find on the Microsoft Store on Windows. These are still two separate Minecraft editions, but the only difference is that you get one or the other when buying Minecraft, and can access both from the launcher.

Best laptops for Minecraft: The bottom line

As you can see, you don’t necessarily need a high-end laptop to play Minecraft, but the more powerful your laptop is, the more you can experience Minecraft. The best laptop for this is the Acer Nitro 5. It strikes the perfect balance of performance and specifications without being too overly expensive. It also has gaming aesthetics, like an RGB keyboard and a fast 144Hz screen.

Source: Acer
Acer Nitro 5
Best overall gaming laptop for Minecraft

Plenty of good features

The Acer Nitro 5 is a very capable gaming laptop for playing Minecraft. It has solid specs out of the box, including 13th-generation Intel CPUs, and a Nvidia GPU that supports Ray Tracing to make your Minecraft worlds more realistic.

$1242 at Amazon

And if you're not into a gaming laptop and want to play Minecraft on a productivity laptop, the Acer Swift Edge 16 will get the job done for you thanks to the AMD Radeon graphics. And we can't forget the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16 Gen 8 either, which is a portable 16-inch gaming laptop. Or, one of the best gaming laptops we reviewed, the HP Omen Transcend 16, with a great display, and a crazy fast CPU and GPU.

Regarding downloads, Java and Bedrock Edition of Minecraft can be downloaded from the Minecraft website while the Windows 10 Edition can be installed from the Microsoft Store or through Xbox Game Pass. If you are interested in more use cases than just playing Minecraft, here is our list of the best laptops that look out over a wider variety of options. And if you are looking for solid mobile connectivity, we've got you covered in our list of the best 5G laptops.

It's not a gaming laptop, but with Minecraft being a lightweight game, this laptop can play it on lower settings without many issues. It has a great design, lots of ports for accessories, and a sleek OLED display with super slim bezels.

Pros
  • Has a great OLED screen
  • Has fast integrated graphics
  • Great design and connectivity
Cons
  • Can get expensive
$1050 at Best Buy

Sometimes, you don't need a laptop with a dedicated GPU to play Minecraft. If you need a laptop for work tasks and don't mind playing on lower settings, the Acer Swift Edge 16 is a great option. This laptop is powered by the AMD Ryzen 7 7840U, which is an eight-core processor. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and 1TB SSD. These specifications are well within the suggested range to run Minecraft, and thanks to the new RDNA3-based Radeon 780M integrated graphics, you shouldn't run into any issues.

The more important thing we want to mention is that this is a productivity laptop, so you can take it with you to school or work. It comes in a basic black color without the bells and whistles you usually see with gaming laptops. It also has a large 16-inch OLED display with slightly slim bezels. OLED panels are more vibrant than regular IPS and LCD panels, so it means your Minecraft worlds will end up looking more life-like.

The ports are what we love about this laptop, though. If you plan to use it at home for gaming or for extra screen space, there's a full-size HDMI port, for connections to an external display. That's in addition to USB-C, USB-A, a microSD card slot, and even a 1440p webcam.

Source: Lenovo
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16 (Gen 8)
Portable 16-inch gaming laptop

Thin but powerful

Lenovo's Legion Slim 5 (Gen 8) is a great laptop for Minecraft. It is under $1,500 and features AMD CPU options, and it has a great design and features like RGB lighting on the keyboard. Most importantly, it's thin but powerful.

$1300 at Lenovo $1530 at Best Buy

If you need a quality but portable 16-inch gaming laptop to play Minecraft look no further than the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16. This laptop sits under Lenovo's Legion Pro devices, but it's still plenty powerful for playing games. It's an AMD-powered laptop, too, that's upgradable. And, it packs in the power of RTX 4060 laptop graphics.

When it comes to the weight and dimensions, this is where this gaming laptop will win out. It comes in at 14.16 inches in length, 10.25 inches in width, and about 0.78 inches in thickness. It's also under 5 pounds. You can easily lug this laptop around, mainly because it's made of plastic. The laptop also has sleek design elements like plastic accents, an intake grille, and an aluminum lid cover.

As for what's under the hood of this laptop, it's AMD Ryzen CPUS. You can go up to an AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS Processor and can add as much as 16GB RAM, and a 1TB SSD. That's also paired with up to an Nvidia RTX 4060 laptop GPU. All of his is above and beyond for a simple game like Minecraft. When we reviewed it, we got up to 79 FPS in games like Red Dead Redemption 2. The display is a huge win, too. It goes up to as much as QHD+ resolution at up to 240 Hz. Such a fast refresh rate means you can stay in the action as you build your worlds and unleash your creativity.

Source: HP
HP Omen Transcend 16 (2023)
The most powerful gaming laptop

All the speed you need

The Omen Transcend 16 (2023) is one of the most expensive gaming laptops, but it's the best at gaming> It also has a 16:10 mini-LED display option and strong CPU performance for games like Minecraft.

Pros
  • Has mini LED display
  • 13th-generation Intel CPUs
  • Great design
Cons
  • Still has a chin on the display
  • Crazy expensive
$1700 at HP $2300 at Best Buy $2050 at Amazon

Our last pick is actually one of the best possible laptops for playing games and for any task, but especially for Minecraft. You'll get the very best gaming experience, and so much more, especially if you're planning to use your laptop for other creative tasks.

For starters, the HP Omen Transcend 16 has one of the higher-end CPUs from Intel. It is up to the Intel Core i9-13900HX which has a crazy 24 Core CPU count. That's paired up with up to 32GB of RAM, and also Nvidia RTX 4060 graphics. When we tested this laptop, we got superfast framerates in all the games on our test library, Advancements in Nvidia's next-generation GPUs like the newer Nvidia Lovelace Architecture only helped the performance of this laptop.

Other than the CPU, you'll really display on this device. The 16:10 aspect ratio, as well as the 2560x1600 resolution panel, will help content look crisp and clear. It shouldn't be an issue for running Minecraft, either. In fact, this is one of the higher-resolution displays on any of the laptops on our list. You can even pick a mini LED panel, which offers more accurate colors.

The overall non-gaming style design, as well as the ports, cap off the reasons why we suggest this device. The HP Poem Transcend 16 has a great keyboard with RGB lighting, but a simplistic toned-down black exterior that's similar to a productivity laptop. As for ports, there are ports on the left and right side of the laptop, as well as the rear. You can easily connect this up to a display or any peripherals you might own thanks to the Thunderbolt 4 ports, HDMU ports, Ethernet ports, and USB-A ports.

What you need to know about Minecraft on PC

There are technically three editions of the game for PC. There is the classic Java Edition of the game, the Bedrock Edition, which expanded support to platforms that don't support Java and Minecraft for Windows. Note that as of June 2022, the Bedrock Edition and the Java Edition have merged into one version, which you'll find on the Microsoft Store on Windows. These are still two separate Minecraft editions, but the only difference is that you get one or the other when buying Minecraft, and can access both from the launcher.

Best laptops for Minecraft: The bottom line

As you can see, you don’t necessarily need a high-end laptop to play Minecraft, but the more powerful your laptop is, the more you can experience Minecraft. The best laptop for this is the Acer Nitro 5. It strikes the perfect balance of performance and specifications without being too overly expensive. It also has gaming aesthetics, like an RGB keyboard and a fast 144Hz screen.

Source: Acer
Acer Nitro 5
Best overall gaming laptop for Minecraft

Plenty of good features

The Acer Nitro 5 is a very capable gaming laptop for playing Minecraft. It has solid specs out of the box, including 13th-generation Intel CPUs, and a Nvidia GPU that supports Ray Tracing to make your Minecraft worlds more realistic.

$1242 at Amazon

And if you're not into a gaming laptop and want to play Minecraft on a productivity laptop, the Acer Swift Edge 16 will get the job done for you thanks to the AMD Radeon graphics. And we can't forget the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 16 Gen 8 either, which is a portable 16-inch gaming laptop. Or, one of the best gaming laptops we reviewed, the HP Omen Transcend 16, with a great display, and a crazy fast CPU and GPU.

Regarding downloads, Java and Bedrock Edition of Minecraft can be downloaded from the Minecraft website while the Windows 10 Edition can be installed from the Microsoft Store or through Xbox Game Pass. If you are interested in more use cases than just playing Minecraft, here is our list of the best laptops that look out over a wider variety of options. And if you are looking for solid mobile connectivity, we've got you covered in our list of the best 5G laptops.

item.title: Best video editors for Chromebook in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best video editors for Chromebook in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
When you think of Chromebooks, creative professionals aren't the first demographic that comes to mind. From the very beginning, ChromeOS acquired a bad reputation for lacking productivity apps. The good news is that ChromeOS has come a long way since its inception.

There are numerous ways you can now do light video editing on your Chromebook. Android apps and web apps both work well for a quick edit. If you want to get more serious, you can also leverage Linux apps to get more serious work done. Let's take a look at the best video editing solutions for your Chromebook.

LumaFusion

Topping the list is LumaFusion, a popular Android video editing app. Google worked closely with the developers behind LumaFusion to ensure that you can use this app to edit your favorite (and professional or semi-professional) videos on your Chromebook. This is a full-on video editor, and it costs $30, but it's also one of the most sophisticated video editors to grace ChromeOS. Similar to iMovie and Final Cut Pro, you have a full timeline, the option to add titles, transitions, graphical effects, and much more in LumaFusion. You even get a project manager to help you manage files.

Video editor in the Google Photos app

Google recently rolled out a new version of the Photos app on ChromeOS that includes a video editor. It isn't a fully featured video editor, though, but it has the basics for quickly sharing personal videos with family and friends. Think of it as a basic video editor for creating montages, or family videos, like the one built into the Windows 11 or Windows 10 Photos app. You can manually control and select video clips and arrange clips, adjust the exposure, brightness, and contrast of those clips, and apply Real Tone filters to adjust skin tones. You can even add a soundtrack if you please.

You can get to this new video editor in ChromeOS' photos app by going to Utilities in the sidebar of the app, and then choosing Movie. There will be a collection of video templates you can choose from that will pull photos from your Google Photos account. You also can manually add photos and videos.

Microsoft Clipchamp

Recently, a new cloud-based and web-based video editor emerged, and it's one owned by Microsoft. Clipchamp is one of the best free web-based video editors to use in 2023 on a Chromebook. The basic tier has unlimited watermark-free exports, and up to 1080p exports, as well as a free library of audio, images, and videos. If you upgrade for $12 a month, you get more premium filters, content backup, brand kits, and other bonus features.

But generally speaking, Clipchamp has basic templates, and controls like trimming videos, speeding up videos, adding lower thirds, animations and much more. Best of all, it's free and easy to use with a lot of great on-screen tutorials. You can use Clipchamp with a Microsoft Account, or Google account, or another emailing service.

Try Clipchamp online

WeVideo web app

WeVideo is cloud-based similar to Clipchamp, so you can make videos anywhere, anytime — even on the go. With WeVideo Jumpstart, there’s zero upload wait time, which means you can create at the speed of inspiration. Don’t have a powerful computer? No problem. WeVideo does all the heavy lifting in the cloud for you no matter what device you’re using.

Not only is WeVideo great for video editing on your Chromebook, but it also works seamlessly across Macs, PCs, and iOS and Android mobile devices. Whether you prefer to make videos on your computer, tablet, or mobile phone, WeVideo makes it a breeze. The plans are also incredibly affordable, with education and business discounts available.

Try WeVideo online

YouTube Studio video editor

YouTube Studio is the official Google app for managing your YouTube channel. The primary purpose of this app is to engage with your audience, check video stats, and manage other channel features. However, you can also do some basic editing in the YouTube Studio web platform.

It's certainly nothing fancy, but you can trim videos, splice clips, and do basic audio editing within YouTube Studio. If you're a small up-and-coming YouTuber and want to get started with editing videos in a basic way, this is a good option to start with.

Try YouTube Studio online

Media.io by Wondershare [Sponsored]

Looking for an AI-enabled, premium alternative that lets you easily edit videos on your Chromebook? Look no further than the sponsor of this segment: Media.io by Wondershare.

What is Media.io? Media.io is an all-in-one web solution from our friends over at Wondershare. Similar to the other online video editing suites above, Media.io is a timeline-based video editor that allows you to manipulate clips and perform various non-destructive changes to your media prior to export. With its intuitive UI and healthy feature set, Media.io makes it easy for you to edit, convert, compress, and share all of your media files on the go.

How is Media.io different? Media.io sets itself apart from many of its competitors is that it packs many AI-enabled tools that streamline your editing workflow. For example, Media.io offers a powerful Vocal Remover feature that can instantly remove vocals from any song, so you can create the perfect background track to any video. It can also automatically transcribe text with its Sound to Text feature, create automatic subtitles with the Auto Subtitle feature, remove image backgrounds with Background Remover, and much more.

How much does Media.io cost? This is the best part. Media.io has plans starting at $0/month. Yes, that’s right, you can upload clips, perform edits, and benefit from all of its AI capabilities without spending a dime. That said, the free plan adds a video watermark and limits the amount you can export, so we’d recommend looking at the Basic or Pro plans if you want to do some serious work on the go. Find the right plan for you on their pricing page.

[We thank Media.io for sponsoring this segment.]

PowerDirector Android app

Whether you’re creating cinematic-style movies, videos of your latest adventure, or combining clips to share exciting, memorable moments, PowerDirector is the perfect app for video editing on a Chromebook. Some of the pro features include trimming, splicing, and rotating videos with simple taps. You also can control brightness, color, and saturation with precision.

Other features include adding text or animated titles to your videos. The paid subscription is a bit expensive, but for the number of features you get, it's worth it. If you want to do a lot of video editing for different social platforms, this is an excellent choice.

KineMaster

KineMaster makes video editing fun on your phone, tablet, or Chromebook. The software makes it easy to edit videos with lots of powerful tools, downloadable content, and much more. Some key features include tools to cut, splice, and crop your videos, combing and editing videos, images, stickers, special effects, text, and more. You even can add music, voiceovers, sound effects, and voice changers.

KineMaster is completely free to use, but you can unlock even more with a KineMaster Premium subscription. A subscription removes the KineMaster watermark and offers an ad-free experience. Learn more by tapping the crown button on the main screen in the app.

Kdenlive

If you need a more powerful video editor, Linux apps are the way to go. There are only two Linux apps I would recommend for video editing on Chrome OS at the moment. Kdenlive is the more full-featured app, but it runs a bit slower in the Chrome OS Linux container. On the other hand, Shotcut lacks a few key features but runs very smoothly on my Chromebook.

Installing Linux apps requires a bit of command-line knowledge. If you aren't familiar with using Terminal commands for Linux, see our complete guide to Linux apps on Chrome OS for an introduction first.

Thankfully, Kdenlive is a nice video editing program for Linux that can run on your Chromebook. Those of you who are used to running Final Cut Pro or Adobe Premiere Pro will pick up the intuitive interface in no time. It's worth noting that while Kdenlive does run well on Chrome OS, you'll need a fairly powerful Chromebook to take full advantage of this app.

Try Kdenlive online

Recapping the best video editors for Chromebooks

Those are our picks for the best video editors available on Chromebooks today. All of these should work well for most folks, but the absolute best experience you'll get is with LumaFusion. The app was developed specially for Android and ChromeOS and is fully featured, without worrying about subscriptions and other recurring fees. Nothing is wrong with our other picks, though. As the Google Photos app will give you a quick and easy way to put together a slideshow video, and you can also try Clipchamp if you want to edit videos online, if your Chromebook isn't powerful enough for Android apps.

As the Google Photos app will give you a quick and easy way to put together a slideshow video, and you can also try Clipchamp if you want to edit videos online, if your Chromebook isn't powerful enough for Android apps.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 deals: How to save on the latest foldable
item.itunes:title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 deals: How to save on the latest foldable
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Samsung's latest flagship foldable is finally on sale, and you can grab it through various retailers or your favorite carriers. While you can no longer utilize the preorder benefits to make the purchase easier on your pocket, there still are a few good deals on the Galaxy Z Fold 5. If you're considering picking one up, check out the Galaxy Z Fold 5 deals below and get a free storage upgrade, enhanced trade-in credit, and other goodies.

Although it's not much of an upgrade over the Galaxy Z Fold 4, the Galaxy Z Fold 5 is a great choice for those looking to buy their first flagship foldable. It even makes sense for those still rocking a Galaxy Z Fold 3 or older. If you fit either one of these categories, order your Galaxy Z Fold 5 today by following the links below and become the proud owner of what is not only one of the best foldable phones but simply one of the year's best phones.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5: Best places to buy the unlocked model

You can buy an unlocked Galaxy Z Fold 5 at various retailers in the U.S., including Samsung's store, Best Buy, and Amazon. Follow the links below to grab an unlocked Galaxy Z Fold 5 at a discount.

Samsung Store

Samsung is offering a free storage upgrade when you purchase an unlocked Galaxy Z Fold 5 in select colors. In addition, you can save up to $1,200 or a minimum of $200 if you trade in your old device. And if you want some accidental damage protection, you can save an additional 30% on a Samsung Care+ plan. Samsung will also throw in four months of free YouTube Premium and Microsoft 365 Basic, along with two months of Adobe Lightroom with each purchase.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

Grab a Galaxy Z Fold 5 from Samsung's store and get a free storage upgrade on select colors, up to $1,200 off with an eligible trade-in, and 30% off on a Samsung Care+ plan.

$1920 at Samsung

Best Buy

You can still get up to $1,000 trade-in savings on your new Galaxy Z Fold 5, but only if you go for the Verizon variant. If you wish to get your device unlocked, you will only be able to get $450 in trade-in savings, but it’s still better than paying full price. You also get a $150 gift card and a one-month Microsoft Xbox Game Pass Ultimate membership. And you can get your new device for as low as $1,400 if you activate your device today.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

You can still save up to $1,000 on a new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 with an eligible trade-in, and you also get a $150 promotional gift card and one free month of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate with your purchase. And you can also score $400 in instant savings if you activate your device today.

$1400 at Best Buy

Amazon

Amazon’s deals for the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 are here, and they’re pretty compelling, as you can now pick up a new model with 256GB storage space for just $1,500, meaning you get to score $300 in instant savings. And the best part is that you will also find the $300 discount applied to the 512GB variant, leaving it up for grabs at $1,620.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

Amazon is a great place to find hot deals and savings, and you can get a $300 discount on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 if you act quickly.

$1500 at Amazon

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5: Best places to buy the carrier-locked model

Get a Galaxy Z Fold 5 from your favorite carrier on a contract and save big on your purchase. The phone is available at all major carriers in the U.S., including AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon.

AT&T

AT&T is letting you get a new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 for as low as $22.23 per month if you choose to get yours on an installment plan, but that’s only possible if you trade in one of your current devices to get $1,000 savings, which will become available in credits over the next 36 months. If you don’t want to trade in your current device, don’t worry; you can still pick one up for $50 per month with $0 down.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

Grab the AT&T variant of the Galaxy Z Fold 5 and get up to $1,000 off with an eligible trade-in.

$1800 at AT&T

T-Mobile

Grab the Galaxy Z Fold 5 through T-Mobile and trade in your old device for up to $1,000 in savings when you add a line on a Go5G Plus or Magenta Max plan. You can also choose to get the device on an installment plan for as low as $33.33 per month with a $0 down payment.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

Get up to $1,000 off with an eligible trade-in when you buy a Galaxy Z Fold 5 from T-Mobile.

$1800 at T-Mobile

Verizon

Shop for the Galaxy Z Fold 5 at Verizon and get up to $1,000 off with an eligible trade-in and a free Galaxy Watch 6 with $350 in promo credit over 36 months. Or you can save on the upfront cost by getting the phone for a monthly payment of $49.99 at a 0% APR. And if you act fast, you can also get up to $449 savings on NFL Sunday Ticket if you activate an eligible Verizon service and redeem the offer within 60 days of purchase, but no later than Dec. 13, 2023.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5

Buy the Galaxy Z Fold 5 through Verizon and get a free Galaxy Watch 6 and up to $1,000 off with an eligible trade-in.

$1800 at Verizon

Save big on the Galaxy Z Fold 5 with these amazing deals

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 is not necessarily the most affordable smartphone on the Android market, but it is undoubtedly one of the coolest and most convenient tools you can get if you’re planning on purchasing a new smartphone that will double up as a productivity tool. It will also be perfect for anyone interested in content creation, as it will let you do more with a larger canvas that supports stylus input. The best part is that these deals will make the phone easy on your pocket, and you can use the extra cash to grab some must-have Galaxy Z Fold 5 accessories.

item.title: How to update the graphics driver on your PC
item.itunes:title: How to update the graphics driver on your PC
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Check what GPU you have
  • How to upgrade AMD graphics drivers
  • How to update Nvidia graphics drivers
  • How to update an Intel graphics driver

Keeping your PC up to date usually helps ensure it runs smoothly. It’s a good habit to periodically check for the latest Windows updates and ensure all system drivers, such as the graphics driver, are up to date. Drivers are pieces of software that help certain hardware communicate with the operating system and other components on your PC.

Graphics drivers are especially important because driver updates add optimizations for new (and old) games as they come out. Many times, graphics cards reviews can even be outdated within a few months, as updates can greatly improve the performance after launch. As such, it's very important to keep these drivers updated.

Check what GPU you have

If you're not sure what graphics card you have, you'll want to check first before starting any downloads. To find out graphics card (or cards) you have, follow these steps:

  1. Right-click the Start menu icon
  2. Select Device Manager.
  3. Expand the Display adapters section to see all your GPUs.

Typically, you'll see one or two GPUs listed here, especially on laptops. Many times, computers have a GPU integrated with the CPU, as well as a discrete GPU, which is the one you'll care about the most for gaming. Note the brand and model you have.

While you can upgrade the graphics driver using Device Manager, this isn't the best method when it comes to getting the latest drivers available. It's easier to stay up to date by downloading drivers directly from the GPU maker.

How to upgrade AMD graphics drivers

There are generally two ways to update your graphics drivers for any of the three brands. You can use the official updater that will always detect the latest version, or you can download the latest version manually when you're ready.

Using AMD Radeon Software

If you already have installed AMD drivers in the past, the AMD Software is the easiest way to keep them up to do it. Simply open AMD software, and if there's a newer driver version available, you'll see it on the home page.

  1. Open AMD Software Adrenalin Edition on your PC.
  2. Click Check for updates near the top right if nothing is shown.
  3. If a new driver is available, click Download.
  4. Once it finishes downloading, click Install to launch the installer.
  5. Click Install again when prompted.
  6. Wait for the process to finish and click Restart when prompted.

You'll now have the latest drivers available. You can repeat this process every few weeks to make sure you're up to date.

Download drivers from AMD's website

To manually download the latest drivers from AMD's website, follow these steps:

  1. Head over to AMD’s download center for drivers.
  2. Click Download Windows drivers near the top to download the automatic driver installer that will detect your hardware and install the right drivers.
  3. Run the installer and click Install.
  4. The installer will detect the drivers compatible with your PC. Select whether you want AMD to collect diagnostic data, then click Next on the graphics drivers screen.
  5. AMD may suggest you install additional tools. Click Skip to ignore them.
  6. Finally, you may see AMD chipset drivers, particularly if you have an AMD CPU. You can click Install if you want them, or Skip if you don't.
  7. Wait a few minutes for the process to finish. It will take longer if you selected the chipset drivers.
  8. Once the installer finishes, click Restart to finish the installation.

Alternatively, you can manually select the drivers for your GPU so you have a full offline installer.

  1. Visit the same AMD download center above.
  2. Using the pane at the bottom to filter for the device you have. The choices will get increasingly specific until you see your product.
  3. Expand the view for your operating system and click Download to get the latest drivers.
  4. Run the installer.
  5. Click Install when prompted.
  6. Wait until the installer finishes, then restart your PC to complete the process.

How to update Nvidia graphics drivers

For Nvidia graphics, you can use the Nvidia GeForce Experience software — usually included with Nvidia drivers, to update your graphics driver whenever a new version is released, but you can also download them manually.

Using GeForce Experience

If you've already installed GeForce Experience with a previous Nvidia driver, you can use it to update your drivers more seamlessly. You can also download GeForce Experience Simply follow these steps:

  1. Launch GeForce Experience on your PC.
  2. Click the Drivers tab.
  3. If an updated driver is available, you'll see a banner at the top of the page. Click Download to get the latest driver.
  4. Once the download finishes, click Express installation to install the driver while keeping all your settings. You can click Custom installation if you want to perform a clean install that deletes your old settings.
  5. Wait for the installation to finish.

Nvidia doesn't require a reboot to finish installing drivers, but it's recommended that you do it anyway.

If you don't have Nvidia GeForce Experience yet, you can download it if you want to automatically detect and install the latest drivers, too.

  1. Head over to Nvidia's GeForce driver download page.
  2. Click Download Now under Automatic driver updates.
  3. Click Agree and install.

Once it finishes installing, you can follow the steps above to automatically get driver updates.

Downloading drivers manually

  1. Go to Nvidia's driver download center.
  2. Select the GPU, operating system and the correct type of drivers. For GeForce GPUs, you'll have Game Ready Driver and Studio Driver options, but there'll be other options depending on the GPU you have.
  3. Click Search to find the latest driver available for your criteria.
  4. Click Download and then click Download again to begin downloading the driver.
  5. Run the installer and follow the steps to extract the Nvidia installer.
  6. When prompted, choose whether to install the Graphics Driver and GeForce Experience or just the driver itself. Click Agree and continue.
  7. Choose whether to use an Express install with default settings or the Custom install if you want to change settings.
  8. If you chose Custom install, choose which components to install. You can also choose to perform a clean installation to remove your existing settings.
  9. Wait for the process to finish and click Close.

Nvidia doesn't force you to restart your PC, but it's always recommended to do so.

How to update an Intel graphics driver

If you have an Intel GPU or processor with integrated graphics, there are a few ways to update its drivers, too. There's Intel Arc Control, the Intel Driver and Software Assistant, and a manual download option.

Using Intel Arc Control

If you have an Intel Arc GPU, you may already have the Intel Arc Control app, which makes it easier to update tour drivers. To use it:

  1. Open Intel Arc Control on your PC.
  2. Click Check for updates in the bottom right corner.
  3. Click Download near the top of the window.
  4. Select Express installation to update your driver seamlessly. You can also click More > Clean to perform a clean installation.
  5. Wait for the driver to finish installing.

Intel Arc Control also gives you options for configuring automatic updates. You can choose how frequent it should check for updates automatically, whether they should be installed automatically, and what driver type you want to install.

Manually download

If your PC doesn't have Arc Control, you can use these steps:

  1. Go to Intel's driver download website.
  2. Click Get started to install the Intel Driver and Software Assistant (DSA) tools.
  3. When the installer finishes, click Launch to open the driver download page.
  4. The page will show any driver updates available for your PC. Select the graphics driver and click Download.
  5. Run the installer.
  6. When you get to this screen, you can click Start for a standard installation or choose Customize to choose the components to install.
  7. (Optional) If you selected Customize, choose the components you want to install. You can also choose to perform a clean installation to remove your existing driver and settings.
  8. Restart your system once the new drivers are installed.

After installing Intel DSA, you can always launch the app to go straight to the driver download page to find the latest drivers for your PC.

Manually download the Intel graphics driver

Finally, if you want to do a fully manual update, you can follow these steps

  1. Go to Intel's driver download center.
  2. Choose Graphics.
  3. Find the right driver for your PC. Most modern PCs will want to find the one called Intel Arc and Irix Xe Graphics - Windows. Click the Download button next to the driver name.

    You can also use the filters on the right side of the screen to find drivers for your specific product if you want to be completely sure it's correct.

  4. Run the installer.
  5. When you get to this screen, you can click Start for a standard installation or choose Customize to choose the components to install.
  6. Once the installation finishes, restart your system to finish installing them

That's all you need to know about updating the graphics driver on your PC. Keep in mind that each manufacturer provides "generic" drivers, and many times, it might be better to use the drivers provided by your PC manufacturer (such as HP, Lenovo, and others). These drivers can contain specific optimizations for your hardware. However, they're also likely to not be updated as frequently, so you'll be missing out on the latest improvements.

Do note that while updating your drivers can help with performance, sometimes hardware is just too old to function as well as you'd like. You may want to check out the best laptops you can buy today if you need more performance. Or, if you're into gaming and you don't want something too expensive, check out the best cheap gaming laptops you can buy right now.
